title: Data, Database Systems, and the Query Journey · Data Contract Brief: Data Custodianship
summary: A support organization has inconsistent spreadsheets, duplicated customer facts, and no defensible retention process. Planned artifact: Add and verify the sql data custodianship increment in a database adoption and data-lifecycle decision record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Classify data purpose, owner, sensitivity, quality risk, retention, and downstream harm before storage.
State the required result as a declarative relation while separating it from execution strategy.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.sql:
-- sql-basics cumulative relational data portfolio.
-- Each increment declares grain, predicts cardinality or changed rows, runs in fresh browser SQLite,
-- reconciles results, tests changed data, and records PostgreSQL or standard-SQL boundaries.

SELECT sqlite_version() AS browser_sqlite_version;

steps.title: Predict Data Custodianship · Theory Data Custodianship
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory support system has 2 spreadsheets, repeated customer identifiers, conflicting ticket status values, and no destruction date. Trace one data item through its lifecycle and the query-processing components before selecting a storage boundary.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Data Custodianship contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict Data Custodianship · Theory Data Custodianship.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for classify data purpose, owner, sensitivity, quality risk, retention, and downstream harm before storage. For Predict Data Custodianship · Theory Data Custodianship, keep Data Custodianship connected to executable relational evidence.

content.title: Non-worked case

steps.title: Build Data Custodianship evidence · Theory Data Custodianship
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable SQLite increment headed "-- Evidence: sql-data-custodianship-87jcr5" with a concrete grain, hypothesis, or prediction; database-system and data-lifecycle evidence; and an observation, reconciliation, or verification tied to returned rows, changed rows, an error, or a plan.

Evidence target: Build Data Custodianship evidence · Theory Data Custodianship.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Data Custodianship into observable performance evidence. For Build Data Custodianship evidence · Theory Data Custodianship, keep Data Custodianship connected to executable relational evidence.

content.code:
-- Evidence: sql-data-custodianship-87jcr5
-- grain: declare the exact row or mutation grain before running
SELECT name, type
FROM sqlite_schema
WHERE type IN ('table', 'view')
ORDER BY type, name;
-- observation: reconcile the returned rows, changed count, error, or plan with the prediction
